rules.title: Complete Diversity
rules.summary: Diversity jurisdiction requires complete diversity between plaintiffs and defendants at filing, plus the amount in controversy threshold.

rules.title: Supplemental Jurisdiction
rules.summary: Supplemental jurisdiction may extend to related claims forming part of the same case or controversy, subject to statutory limits.

rules.title: Minimum Contacts
rules.summary: Specific jurisdiction requires purposeful contacts and fairness consistent with due process.

rules.title: General Jurisdiction
rules.summary: General jurisdiction ordinarily exists where an individual is domiciled or a corporation is essentially at home.

rules.title: State Substantive Law
rules.summary: A federal court sitting in diversity generally applies state substantive law and federal procedural law.

rules.title: Claim Preclusion
rules.summary: A final judgment on the merits can bar later claims arising from the same transaction against the same opposing party.

rules.title: Issue Preclusion
rules.summary: Issue preclusion can prevent relitigation of an issue actually litigated and necessarily decided in a prior action.
